description_long: This object is a vintage wooden turntable radio console, featuring a hinged lid that opens to reveal a record player on top. The exterior is finished in a rich, dark wood veneer with a glossy sheen, and the front panel includes a fabric speaker grille and two large rotary knobs for tuning and volume control. Below the speaker grille, there is a glass-covered frequency display, likely for AM/FM radio tuning. The sides of the console have decorative cutouts with mesh, possibly for additional speaker output or ventilation, and a power button is visible on one side. The back is plain wood, with no visible controls or openings. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century home audio furniture, blending both electronic and decorative elements.
description: A vintage wooden turntable radio console with a hinged lid, fabric speaker grille, rotary knobs, and a glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open lid, record player, fabric speaker grille, rotary knobs, and frequency display.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the open lid, side speaker cutouts with mesh, and a power button.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, glossy wooden back with no visible controls or openings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the open lid and matching decorative speaker cutouts with mesh.
